data.title: State of Wisconsin Investment Board reports over $321M BTC exposure
data.description:
The State of Wisconsin Investment Board, the entity overseeing the state’s pension fund, increased its Bitcoin exposure to around $321 million, according to a Feb. 14 filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission.

Wisconsin’s pension fund previously disclosed holding $164 million in Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) in a May 2024 SEC filing.

At the time, the pension fund held approximately 2.4 million shares of BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT), valued at $100 million, and 1 million shares of Grayscale’s Bitcoin Trust (GBTC), valued at $64 million.

The pension fund’s most recent filing indicates that the fund has allocated all of its BTC exposure into IBIT and no longer holds any shares of GBTC.

The State of Wisconsin Investment Board’s increased Bitcoin exposure highlights a growing trend among pension funds allocating a portion of their assets to Bitcoin as a hedge against currency inflation and a diversified portfolio.

Pension funds adopt Bitcoin to protect purchasing power

Although Bitcoin is notorious for its high volatility, pension funds have a long time horizon for their investments that allows them to capture long-term price appreciation while ignoring short-term price movements.

Attorney Allie Itami of Lathrop GPM told Cointelegraph that state pension funds could adopt crypto more easily than privately managed funds due to fiduciary restrictions under the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA) of 1974.

Following Wisconsin’s May 2024 filing disclosing BTC exposure, several state pension funds also disclosed BTC ETF holdings.

In July 2024, the State of Michigan Retirement System reported $6.6 million in BTC exposure, which is only a tiny fraction of the multibillion-dollar fund’s assets under management.

Florida chief financial officer Jimmy Patronis wrote a letter to the Florida State Board of Administration — the state’s pension manager — urging the fund to allocate a portion of its assets to BTC in October 2024.

More recently, in February 2025, North Carolina House of Representatives speaker Destin Hall introduced a bill to allow the state treasury to invest in digital assets indirectly by holding ETFs.

data.title: Daily Roundup of Key US Economic Data for Feb. 14
data.description:
Retail sales fell by 0.9% in January and were down 0.4% excluding a 2.8% drop in auto sales, below estimates for both metrics.

A 4.6% drop in sporting goods sales and a 1.7% drop in furniture sales were the key factors, offset by sales gains at restaurants and department stores. Control group retail sales, which exclude motor vehicles, gas, building materials, and food services, fell by 0.8% after a 0.9% gain in December.

Import prices rose by 0.3% in January and were still up 0.1% excluding a 3.2% gain in fuel prices. Petroleum prices were up 2.9%. Export prices increased by 1.3% in January and were up 1.5% excluding a 0.2% decline in agricultural prices.

January industrial production rose by 0.5% after a 1% gain in December, with manufacturing production down 0.1% overall but up 0.2% excluding a 5.2% drop in motor vehicle and parts production. Utilities output surged by 7.2%, lifted by the demand for heat due to cold weather. Mining production fell by 1.2%.

Business inventories fell by 0.2% in December, below expectations and following a 0.1% increase in November. Business sales rose by 0.8%, with gains at the factory, wholesale, and retail levels.

The Q1 GDPnow estimate from the Atlanta Fed is for a 2.3% gain, revised down from a 2.9% gain reported on Feb. 7. The next update is scheduled for Feb. 19.

The St. Louis Fed's initial GDP nowcast estimate for Q1 is for a 2.11% gain.

data.title: Chemed Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.50 a Share, Payable March 14 to Holders of Record as of Feb. 24
TSMC is receptive to Intel factory deal
data.description:
TSMC is reportedly interested in an Intel factory deal.

The company is weighing taking a controlling stake in partnership and considering operating US Inte factories after a request from US Pres. Trump.

Other tech firms may also take a stake in possible deal.

Intel is currently without a CEO after the former CEO Pat Gelsinger was ousting back in December. At the time Frank Yeary, Intel Board chair said that “We are working to create a leaner, simpler, more agile Intel,”.

A deal like this would be a simpler, more agile Intel.

Shares of Intel traded as high as $25.19 shortly after the open today, but then fell to a low of $22.86. The price spiked to $24.50 on the news headlines but has since moved down to $23.73 currently.

Both Intel and TSMC are building new fabs in Arizona funded in part from the Chips Act.

data.title: NEXIEN BIOPHARMA, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Nexien BioPharma, Inc., a company focused on the pre-clinical and drug development activities for pharmaceutical formulations that include cannabinoids, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ending December 31, 2024. The report provides insights into the company's financial performance and operational challenges, underscoring the need for additional capital to sustain its business activities.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Loss: $39,472 for the three months ended December 31, 2024, an increase in loss of $14,954 from the net loss of $24,518 reported for the three months ended December 31, 2023.
  • Net Loss: $71,476 for the six months ended December 31, 2024, a decrease in loss of $27,960 from the net loss of $99,436 reported for the six months ended December 31, 2023.
  • Loss per share - basic and diluted: $0.00 for both the three and six months ended December 31, 2024 and 2023.
  • Professional fees: $16,020 for the three months ended December 31, 2024, increased by $10,395 from $5,625 for the period ended December 31, 2023.
  • Professional fees: $28,700 for the six months ended December 31, 2024, increased by $7,535 from $21,165 for the period ended December 31, 2023.
  • General and administrative expenses: $8,007 for the three months ended December 31, 2024, compared to $7,811 for the same period in 2023.
  • General and administrative expenses: $11,037 for the six months ended December 31, 2024, compared to $54,266 for the same period in 2023, with the 2023 figure including $35,000 in non-cash stock-based compensation costs.
  • Interest expense: $11,131 for the three months ended December 31, 2024, compared to $7,521 for the same period in 2023.
  • Interest expense: $21,381 for the six months ended December 31, 2024, compared to $15,042 for the same period in 2023.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Nexien BioPharma, Inc. is focused on pre-clinical and drug development activities for pharmaceutical formulations that include cannabinoids. The company is pursuing the formulation and development of drugs containing cannabinoids for the treatment of various diseases, disorders, and medical conditions.
  • Intellectual Property and Licensing: The company has filed three provisional patent applications and acquired a license covering certain intellectual property related to a drug delivery system. This includes a worldwide exclusive license for a proprietary delivery system for cannabinoid-based medications obtained from Accu-Break Pharmaceuticals Inc.
  • Operational Challenges: Nexien BioPharma has not established any source of revenue to cover its operating costs and has incurred an operating loss since inception. The company had to cease research and development activities due to a lack of sufficient working capital.
  • Future Outlook: The company requires significant additional capital to fund its business plan, which includes research, regulatory applications, and clinical trials. Management is actively seeking merger or other business combination or restructuring opportunities to address the financial challenges.
  • Management's Focus: The management is concentrating on raising additional capital and exploring strategic arrangements with third parties to sustain operations and potentially resume research and development activities.
  • Regulatory Pathway: The development of pharmaceuticals with the objective of obtaining approval by the FDA and other international regulatory authorities is a long-term endeavor that requires substantial capital funding.
  • Capital Requirements: Nexien BioPharma estimates a need to raise at least $50,000 to maintain its existence as a public company for the remainder of the current fiscal year, highlighting the urgency of securing additional funding.

SEC Filing: NEXIEN BIOPHARMA, INC. [ NXEN ] - 10-Q - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: TSMC considering running Intel's US factories after Trump team request, Bloomberg reports
data.description:
Taiwanese contract manufacturer TSMC is considering taking a controlling stake in chipmaker Intel's factories at the request of U.S. President Donald Trump's officials, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

Trump's team raised the idea of a deal between the two companies in recent meetings with officials from TSMC, and the Taiwanese company was receptive, Bloomberg reported, citing a person familiar with the matter.

It is unclear whether Intel is open to a transaction, according to the report.

The talks are in very early stages, and the exact structure of a potential partnership has not been established, but the intended result would have TSMC fully operating Intel's U.S. semiconductor factories, according to the report.

TSMC declined to comment.

data.title: Sunoco LP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Sunoco LP, a Delaware master limited partnership primarily engaged in energy infrastructure and the distribution of motor fuels, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, significant business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces in the evolving energy market.

Financial Highlights

Sunoco LP reported strong financial results for 2024, reflecting its extensive operations in energy infrastructure and distribution:

  • Revenues: $21,912 million, showcasing the company's expansive reach across multiple regions.
  • Gross Profit: $1,734 million, driven by acquisitions and improved segment performance.
  • Operating Income: $667 million, indicating strong operational efficiency despite increased expenses from recent acquisitions.
  • Net Income: $874 million, significantly boosted by a $586 million gain from the West Texas Sale.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not explicitly stated in the report.

Business Highlights

Sunoco LP's business operations in 2024 were marked by significant achievements and expansions:

  • Business Overview: Sunoco LP operates an extensive network of over 14,000 miles of pipeline and more than 100 terminals, distributing motor fuels across over 40 U.S. states, Puerto Rico, Europe, and Mexico.
  • Segment Operations: The company is divided into three reportable segments: Fuel Distribution, Pipeline Systems, and Terminals, each contributing to the overall operations with specific roles in distribution, transportation, and storage.
  • Fuel Distribution Segment: Sunoco is a major distributor of motor fuels and other petroleum products, supplying approximately 5,619 company and third-party operated locations in the U.S. and Puerto Rico.
  • Pipeline Systems Segment: This segment includes an integrated pipeline and terminal network with approximately 6,000 miles of refined product pipeline, 6,000 miles of crude oil pipeline, and 2,000 miles of ammonia pipeline.
  • Terminals Segment: Sunoco operates four transmix processing facilities and 56 refined product terminals, including two in Europe, six in Hawaii, and 48 in the continental U.S.
  • Geographical Performance: The company expanded its operations internationally with acquisitions in Europe, enhancing its supply optimization for the East Coast business.
  • Sales Units: In 2024, Sunoco sold 8,578 million gallons of motor fuel, marking an increase from the previous year.
  • New Acquisitions: Significant acquisitions in 2024 included NuStar Energy L.P. and Zenith European terminals, enhancing the company's midstream income.
  • Divestitures: The company sold 204 convenience stores in West Texas, New Mexico, and Oklahoma to 7-Eleven, Inc.
  • Joint Ventures: Sunoco formed ET-S Permian, a joint venture with Energy Transfer, combining crude oil and produced water gathering assets in the Permian Basin.
  • Future Outlook: Sunoco expects to continue diversifying its business, increasing scale, and expanding cash for reinvestment and distribution growth through strategic transactions and organic growth opportunities.

Strategic Initiatives

Sunoco LP undertook several strategic initiatives in 2024 to enhance its business and financial standing:

  • Strategic Acquisitions: The company completed acquisitions of NuStar Energy L.P. and liquid fuels terminals from Zenith Energy to diversify its business and enhance vertical integration.
  • Joint Ventures: The formation of ET-S Permian with Energy Transfer aims to increase operational scale and improve the company's credit profile.
  • Capital Management: Sunoco issued $750 million of 7.000% senior notes due 2029 and $750 million of 7.250% senior notes due 2032 to repay NuStar's outstanding debt and fund the redemption of NuStar's preferred units. The company also managed its liquidity through a Credit Facility with a borrowing capacity of $1.25 billion and paid $566 million in distributions to unitholders.
  • Future Outlook: Sunoco plans to continue its strategic focus on growth through acquisitions and capital investments, with expected capital expenditures of at least $400 million in growth capital for 2025. The company aims to leverage its expanded asset base to optimize supply and enhance cash flow for reinvestment and distribution growth.

Challenges and Risks

Sunoco LP faces several challenges and risks that could impact its operations and financial performance:

  • Market Risks: Volatility in the prices of motor fuel, crude oil, and refined petroleum products can adversely affect margins and customer financial conditions. The transition to a low carbon economy poses a significant threat to demand for traditional petroleum products.
  • Operational Risks: The company is exposed to risks inherent in the storage and transportation of hazardous materials. Reliance on third-party pipelines and facilities could disrupt operations if unavailable.
  • Regulatory Risks: New environmental regulations could impose additional costs and accelerate the transition away from fossil fuels. Compliance with stringent federal, state, and local laws could require substantial expenditures.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: Increasingly sophisticated cybersecurity attacks pose a threat to the company's IT infrastructure, potentially leading to operational disruptions and financial losses.
  • Climate Change Risks: Physical risks associated with climate change, such as severe weather events, could damage infrastructure and disrupt supply chains. Regulatory and societal pressures to reduce greenhouse gas emissions may also impact operations and financial performance.

Management is actively working on strategies to mitigate these risks, including investing in cybersecurity measures, exploring opportunities in renewable energy, and enhancing operational resilience through strategic partnerships and infrastructure improvements.

SEC Filing: Sunoco LP [ SUN ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: PRICE T ROWE GROUP INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
T. Rowe Price Group Inc., a leading global investment management firm, has released its 2024 10-K report, showcasing robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and addressing key challenges and risks. The report highlights the company's continued growth in assets under management, strategic investments in technology and personnel, and its focus on expanding its global presence.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Revenues: $7,093.6 million, increased by 9.8% from 2023 due to higher average assets under management and stronger market returns.
  • Operating Income: $2,333.3 million, increased by 17.5% from 2023, driven by net revenue growth outpacing operating expense growth.
  • Net Income to T. Rowe Price Group: $2,100.1 million, increased by 17.4% from 2023, primarily due to higher operating income and a lower effective tax rate.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $9.15, increased by 17.9% from 2023, reflecting higher net income and a lower effective tax rate.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: T. Rowe Price Group's investment advisory fees are primarily derived from four main asset classes: equity, fixed income (including money markets), multi-asset, and alternatives. The multi-asset class, which includes target date retirement products, has shown significant growth, contributing to a substantial portion of the firm's assets under management.
  • Geographical Performance: The company services clients in 54 countries, with investors outside the U.S. representing about 9% of total assets under management at the end of 2024. The EMEA and APAC regions experienced net inflows, although these were outweighed by outflows in the Americas.
  • Sales Units: As of December 31, 2024, T. Rowe Price managed $1,606.6 billion in assets, with a notable increase driven by market appreciation and income. The target date retirement products alone accounted for $475.6 billion, or 29.6% of the total managed assets.
  • New Product Launches: The firm continues to introduce new strategies and investment vehicles to complement and expand its offerings, responding to competitive developments and client needs. This includes specialized advisory services and new investment products.
  • Future Outlook: T. Rowe Price is focused on sustaining its leadership in retirement, accessing growth in the U.S. wealth management channel, and expanding its global presence in select high-opportunity countries. The firm is also enhancing its distribution technology and client service capabilities to strengthen its competitive position.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Investments: The company is investing in key capabilities, including hiring investment and distribution professionals, enhancing technologies, and developing new product offerings to provide strong investment management expertise and service. Additionally, the company is focusing on strategic investment advice solutions, including strategic asset allocation and tactical asset allocation overlays, primarily overseen by the multi-asset division.
  • Capital Management: During 2024, the company increased its stockholders’ equity from $9.5 billion to $10.3 billion and its tangible book value to $7.5 billion. It paid $4.96 per share in regular dividends, a 1.6% increase from 2023, and repurchased nearly 3.0 million shares for $334.5 million. The company has returned $4.8 billion to stockholders since the end of 2021 through stock repurchases and dividends. The company maintains ample liquidity with $5,480.1 million in cash and investments, including $2,649.8 million in cash and cash equivalents.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its strategic investments in technology and personnel to support growth and enhance service offerings. It anticipates property and equipment expenditures of about $300 million in 2025, primarily for technology initiatives. The company also plans to maintain its dividend policy and share repurchase program to manage capital effectively. Additionally, the company is committed to funding OHA product launches with $360 million remaining to be committed through 2026.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: Fluctuations in financial markets can significantly affect the value of assets under management, impacting revenues. The trend towards passive investment products continues to pressure active management market share.
  • Operational Risks: The complexity of operations, reliance on third-party service providers, and the need for robust technology systems present risks. Failures in these areas could harm the company's reputation and financial results.
  • Regulatory Risks: The firm faces an increasingly complex regulatory environment, with heightened focus on ESG disclosures, data protection, and fiduciary responsibilities. Non-compliance could result in fines and damage to reputation.
  • Emerging Risks: Climate change poses both physical and transition risks, potentially affecting asset values and client preferences. The use of AI technology introduces risks related to data privacy and regulatory compliance.
  • Geopolitical Risks: Investments in regions with political instability, such as the ongoing conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East, could adversely affect asset values.

SEC Filing: PRICE T ROWE GROUP INC [ TROW ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: Energy Transfer LP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Energy Transfer LP, a leading provider of natural gas, crude oil, and NGL transportation and storage services, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, significant business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Energy Transfer LP reported impressive financial results for 2024, showcasing significant growth across key metrics:

  • Total Revenue: $95,007 million, reflecting a significant increase driven by higher volumes and contributions from recently acquired assets.
  • Net Income: $6,565 million, an increase of approximately 24% from the previous year, primarily due to a gain on asset sales and the absence of prior year litigation-related losses.
  • Adjusted EBITDA (consolidated): $15,483 million, up by approximately 13%, supported by favorable results across multiple segments, particularly midstream and crude oil transportation.
  • Depreciation, Depletion and Amortization: $5,165 million, increased due to additional depreciation from new assets and acquisitions.
  • Interest Expense, Net of Interest Capitalized: $3,125 million, reflecting higher debt balances and increased interest rates.

Business Highlights

Energy Transfer LP's business operations demonstrated strong performance and strategic growth in 2024:

  • Natural Gas Operations: The company operates one of the largest intrastate pipeline systems in the United States, with approximately 12,200 miles of intrastate natural gas transportation pipelines and 20,090 miles of interstate natural gas pipelines. The company provides transportation and storage services with a capacity of 24 Bcf/d for intrastate and 20.1 Bcf/d for interstate operations.
  • Crude Oil and NGL Operations: Energy Transfer owns and operates approximately 17,950 miles of crude oil trunk and gathering pipelines and 5,700 miles of NGL pipelines. Key assets include the Bakken Pipeline, Permian Express Pipelines, and the ET-S Permian joint venture, which collectively enhance crude oil and NGL transportation capabilities.
  • Significant Acquisitions: In 2024, Energy Transfer completed the acquisition of WTG Midstream, adding approximately 6,000 miles of gas gathering pipelines and eight gas processing plants, extending its network into the Midland Basin.
  • Joint Ventures: The formation of ET-S Permian, a joint venture with Sunoco LP, combines crude oil and produced water gathering assets in the Permian Basin, operating over 5,000 miles of pipelines with a storage capacity exceeding 11 million barrels.
  • LNG Operations: Lake Charles LNG, a wholly owned subsidiary, operates an LNG import terminal and regasification facility with a storage capacity of 9.0 Bcf and a send-out capacity of 1.8 Bcf/d. The company is developing a liquefaction project at the same site, with ongoing discussions for long-term LNG offtake agreements.
  • Midstream Segment: The midstream segment focuses on natural gas gathering, compression, treating, and processing, with operations concentrated in major producing basins and shales across the United States. The segment has an aggregate processing capacity of approximately 12.9 Bcf/d.
  • NGL and Refined Products: The NGL and refined products segment includes significant assets such as the Mont Belvieu NGL Complex, with a fractionation capacity of 1.15 MMBbls/d and storage capacity of 62 MMBbls. The segment also includes the Marcus Hook Terminal, which supports export activities.
  • Crude Oil Terminals: Key crude oil terminals include the Nederland Terminal with a storage capacity of 30 MMBbls, the Houston Terminal with 18.2 MMBbls, and the Cushing Terminal with 9.5 MMBbls, providing extensive storage and distribution capabilities.
  • Future Outlook: Energy Transfer plans to continue strategic acquisitions, expand its infrastructure, and increase cash flow from fee-based businesses to support growth and maintain investment-grade credit ratings.

Strategic Initiatives

Energy Transfer LP has undertaken several strategic initiatives to bolster its market position and financial stability:

  • Strategic Acquisitions: In 2024, Energy Transfer completed the acquisition of WTG Midstream for $2.28 billion in cash and 50.8 million newly issued common units, and Sunoco LP's acquisition of NuStar and Zenith European terminals. These acquisitions expanded Energy Transfer's network and capacity, particularly in the Midland Basin and international markets.
  • Joint Ventures: The formation of ET-S Permian, a joint venture with Sunoco LP, combines crude oil and produced water gathering assets in the Permian Basin.
  • Capital Management: Energy Transfer engaged in significant capital management activities, including issuing $1.25 billion in senior notes and redeeming various series of preferred units to optimize its capital structure. The company also increased its debt level by $4.74 billion in 2024 to support acquisitions and capital expenditures. Energy Transfer paid $4.62 billion in distributions to partners and $1.78 billion to noncontrolling interests. Sunoco LP issued $1.5 billion in senior notes to refinance NuStar's debt and fund the redemption of NuStar's preferred units.
  • Future Outlook: Energy Transfer anticipates a strong outlook supported by increased production and utilization of existing assets, as well as continued demand for its products. The company plans to invest approximately $5 billion in growth capital expenditures and $1.1 billion in maintenance capital expenditures in 2025. Energy Transfer expects to maintain ample liquidity and access to debt capital markets, with a focus on prudent capital allocation across its business segments. The company also plans to continue developing alternative energy projects to reduce its environmental footprint.

Challenges and Risks

Energy Transfer LP faces several challenges and risks that could impact its business operations and financial performance:

  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to fluctuations in the demand for and price of natural gas, NGLs, crude oil, and refined products. This volatility is influenced by global economic conditions, geopolitical events, and changes in energy conservation efforts. The Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 could also decrease demand for fossil fuels and impose new costs on operations.
  • Operational Risks: The company is vulnerable to natural disasters, cybersecurity breaches, and supply chain disruptions. Extreme weather events and competition for water resources for hydraulic fracturing could disrupt operations. Additionally, the company faces challenges in maintaining and acquiring rights-of-way for pipeline operations, which are critical for business continuity.
  • Regulatory Risks: Increased regulation of hydraulic fracturing and produced water disposal could lead to reduced crude oil and natural gas production. Legal actions related to the Dakota Access Pipeline and changes in U.S. trade policies, including tariffs on steel, could adversely affect project costs and operations. The company is also subject to evolving cybersecurity and data privacy laws, which may require significant investment to ensure compliance.
  • Emerging Risks: The company is impacted by the Federal Reserve's monetary policy, which has led to significant interest rate increases, affecting the cost of capital and potentially reducing demand for storage capacity. The Biden Administration's moratorium on LNG export authorizations and subsequent legal challenges introduce uncertainty in the company's liquefaction project.

Management has identified several strategies to address these challenges, including enhancing cybersecurity measures, exploring alternative financing options, and engaging in strategic acquisitions and pipeline construction projects to support future growth.

SEC Filing: Energy Transfer LP [ ET ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

  • Year-to-date it is up 67.05 cents or 16.82%

All prices are calculated based on the settlement price of the current front month contract.

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: Update: NeuroPace Prices $65 Million Public Offering; Shares Jump
data.description:
(Updates with the stock movement in the headline and the last paragraph.)

NeuroPace said Friday it has priced a public offering of 6.5 million shares at $10 per share.

Gross proceeds from the offering, expected to close on Feb. 18, are expected to be $65 million, the company said.

NeuroPace said it has also granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 975,000 additional shares.

The company said it expects to use around $49.5 million of net proceeds from the offering to acquire 5.3 million shares held by KCK at $9.40 per share, and the remainder for general corporate purposes.

Shares of the company were up more than 8% in recent Friday trading.

data.title: Toll Brothers Announces Model Home for Sale at Diamond Canyon Community in Pleasanton, California
data.description:
PLEASANTON, Calif., Feb. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Toll Brothers, Inc. , the nation’s leading builder of luxury homes, today announced the final home at its Diamond Canyon community in Pleasanton, California is now available for sale. The community’s stunning Dresden Farmhouse model home, located at 1201 Lund Ranch Road in Pleasanton, has been listed for sale at $4,999,995 and is available to view by appointment.

This exclusive community is nestled within an established neighborhood in Pleasanton, featuring estate-sized single-family homes on expansive home sites. The Dresden Farmhouse model home features 5 bedrooms, 5.5 bathrooms, and a 3-car garage with over 4,500 square feet of living space. This award-winning move-in ready home is designer-decorated and fully landscaped with a vast array of high-end finishes and structural options, providing a unique opportunity to own this meticulously crafted home.

“This is the last chance for home buyers to own a luxury home in the highly sought-after Diamond Canyon community,” said Alli Sweeney, Division President of Toll Brothers in Northern California. “The Dresden Farmhouse model home offers an exceptional opportunity to own a move-in ready Toll Brothers signature home in a prime location.”

Residents of Diamond Canyon enjoy Blue-Ribbon award-winning public and private schools within the Pleasanton Unified School District. The community is conveniently located near downtown Pleasanton, offering local shopping, restaurants, arts and culture, golf courses, parks, and outdoor spaces. Easy access to the 680 and 580 Freeways and the Pleasanton/Dublin BART station makes commuting a breeze.

For more information, prospective home buyers are invited to call 844-790-5263 or visit TollBrothers.com/NorCal.

About Toll Brothers

Toll Brothers, Inc., a Fortune 500 Company, is the nation’s leading builder of luxury homes. The Company was founded 58 years ago in 1967 and became a public company in 1986. Its common stock is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol “TOL.” The Company serves first-time, move-up, empty-nester, active-adult, and second-home buyers, as well as urban and suburban renters. Toll Brothers builds in over 60 markets in 24 states: Arizona, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Idaho, Indiana, Maryland, Massachusetts, Michigan, Nevada, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Oregon, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, and Washington, as well as in the District of Columbia. The Company operates its own architectural, engineering, mortgage, title, land development, smart home technology, and landscape subsidiaries. The Company also develops master-planned and golf course communities as well as operates its own lumber distribution, house component assembly, and manufacturing operations.

In 2024, Toll Brothers marked 10 years in a row being named to the Fortune World’s Most Admired Companies™ list and the Company’s Chairman and CEO Douglas C. Yearley, Jr. was named one of 25 Top CEOs by Barron’s magazine. Toll Brothers has also been named Builder of the Year by Builder magazine and is the first two-time recipient of Builder of the Year from Professional Builder magazine. For more information visit TollBrothers.com.

From Fortune, ©2024 Fortune Media IP Limited. All rights reserved. Used under license.

Contact: Andrea Meck | Toll Brothers, Senior Director, Public Relations & Social Media | 215-938-8169 | ameck@tollbrothers.com

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/ece7108e-3156-49df-a83b-0e4dff3f027a

Sent by Toll Brothers via Regional Globe Newswire (TOLL-REG)

Diamond Canyon by Toll Brothers

The Dresden Farmhouse model home is for sale in Toll Brothers Diamond Canyon luxury home community in Pleasanton, California.

data.title: Musk's xAI in talks to raise $10 billion at $75 billion valuation, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Elon Musk's artificial intelligence startup xAI is discussing raising $10 billion at a valuation of $75 billion, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

Existing investors, including Sequoia Capital, Andreessen Horowitz and Valor Equity Partners are in talks to participate in the deal, the report said.

The AI startup said in December it had raised about $6 billion in equity financing.

data.title: LA Galaxy Players Edwin Cerrillo and Mauricio Cuevas Empower Coachella Valley Migrant Students
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / February 14, 2025 / With the AEG's Coachella Valley Invitational underway, LA Galaxy players Edwin Cerrillo and Mauricio Cuevas took a break from pre-season matches on February 10, 2025, to meet with migrant students at Palm View Elementary School in Coachella, CA, as part of the school's Migrant Education program. Hosted by the LA Galaxy Foundation, the event provided students with a unique day of mentorship and soccer skill-building.

During the event, Cerrillo and Cuevas participated in a heartfelt conversation with LA Galaxy broadcaster, Joe Tutino, where they shared their personal journeys from youth soccer to the professional stage, and discussed how they overcame the challenges they faced and stayed committed to their dreams.

"Today's event was about fostering confidence and inspiring the next generation," said Gabriel Osollo, Director, Community Relations and LA Galaxy Foundation. "By engaging with their role models in such a personal way, the students gained a sense of empowerment and encouragement to chase their dreams, while also improving their soccer skills."

Following the panel, Martha Saucedo, Chief External Affairs Officer of AEG, presented the Coachella Valley Unified School District's Migrant Program with a check donation in the amount of $5,000. After the presentation, Cerrillo and Cuevas led students through skill-building drills, sharing professional tips and providing hands-on coaching with the students.

The Coachella Valley Unified School District's Migrant Program is dedicated to ensuring students from migrant families achieve academic excellence and graduate with a high school diploma. To learn more about the program, please click here.

The LA Galaxy Foundation, the 501(c)(3) organization associated with the LA Galaxy, is dedicated to assisting the community in which the team lives, works, and plays. LA Galaxy players, coaches, alumni and staff spend countless hours giving back to the community at local schools, hospitals, fields, and events. The LAGF continually strives to aid organizations that meet its mission of supporting and creating education, health, and soccer-related activities and programs for youth in the greater Los Angeles area. To learn more about the LA Galaxy Foundation, click here.

Cerrillo and Cuevas participated in a heartfelt conversation with LA Galaxy broadcaster, Joe Tutino.

View additional multimedia and more ESG storytelling from AEG on 3blmedia.com.

data.title: Was the emergence of intelligent life on Earth just a fluke? Some scientists think not
data.description:
Roughly 300,000 years ago, our species first appeared on the African landscape before spreading globally and coming to dominate the planet. All this happened about 4.5 billion years after Earth formed, with innumerable steps occurring in between that made our planet a cradle for intelligent life.

An influential scientific thesis - called the "hard steps" theory and first presented in 1983 - has held that this outcome was a long shot and that the emergence of technological-level intelligent life on Earth or elsewhere was highly improbable. But perhaps this result was not so unlikely after all, according to scientists who are now advancing an alternative theory.

These scientists propose that Homo sapiens and analogous extraterrestrial life forms may be the probable end result of biological and planetary evolution when a planet has a certain set of attributes that make it habitable, rather than requiring countless lucky breaks.

The path toward intelligent life, they argue, may be more of a predictable process, unfolding as global conditions allow in a manner that should not be considered unique to Earth.

"In short, our framework shows how hard steps may not actually exist - past evolutionary transitions that needed to happen for us humans to be here may not have been hard or unlikely in the available time," said Dan Mills, a postdoctoral researcher in geomicrobiology at the University of Munich and lead author of the study published on Friday in the journal Science Advances.

Physicist Brandon Carter devised the hard steps theory. It stresses that the long road to the emergence of humankind necessitated passage through various intermediate steps, each highly unlikely.

Over the years, scientists have tried to identify some of these hard steps. These include the emergence of single-celled living organisms on primordial Earth, the initial oxygenation of the atmosphere by photosynthesis, the evolutionary transition from prokaryotic cells that lack a nucleus and other internal structures to eukaryotic cells that have them, and the appearance of complex organisms such as multicellular animals. And then, the final proposed hard step is the appearance of Homo sapiens and milestones such as language and technology.

A species with advanced technological capabilities emerged on Earth relatively late in the Earth's habitable history, with the sun expected to increase in luminosity and boil away our planet's oceans about a billion years from now. This has inspired the argument that Earth is an incredibly rare planet that managed to accomplish the needed hard steps before becoming rendered uninhabitable.

The new theory was devised by a team of two geobiologists and two astronomers.

They propose that humankind's emergence followed the sequential opening of various "windows of habitability" over Earth's history, driven by factors such as changes in nutrient availability, sea surface temperatures, ocean salinity levels and atmospheric oxygen levels. Due to these factors, Earth only relatively recently became hospitable to a species like ours, they said, and that once those conditions existed the evolutionary path was relatively rapid.

"Biological innovations proposed as hard or unlikely might actually occur quickly - geologically speaking - as soon as the environment permits," said Penn State microbiologist Jennifer Macalady, one of the researchers.

"For example, life might have originated very quickly once temperatures were appropriate for the stability of biomolecules and liquid water. The Earth has only been habitable for humans since the second rise of oxygen in the atmosphere approximately 0.5 billion years ago, meaning that humans could not have evolved on Earth prior to that relatively recent moment," Macalady added.

Astronomers are searching for evidence of life beyond Earth and have identified roughly 5,800 exoplanets - planets beyond our solar system. Some of them are uninhabitable gas giants akin to Jupiter but some of them are rocky worlds like our solar system's four terrestrial planets that include Earth.

Astrophysicist and study co-author Jason Wright, director of the Penn State Extraterrestrial Intelligence Center, said that a best estimate right now is that somewhere around half of stars have a planet about the size of Earth orbiting at about the right distance to host liquid water, a key ingredient for life.

"Understanding the probability of intelligent life emerging helps us understand our own place in the world," Mills said. "Are we humans a cosmic fluke, as the hard steps model predicts? Or are we instead the more expected and typical outcome of a living planet, as our alternative framework suggests?"

data.title: Brazil's Vale confirms $12 billion investment in Carajas complex through 2030
data.description:
Brazilian miner Vale confirmed on Friday that it will invest 70 billion reais ($12.26 billion) in its Carajas complex in northern Brazil through 2030.

Brazil's presidential palace had initially announced the planned investment on Wednesday. Vale added in a statement that the expected investments toward the "New Carajas" plan are in line with its guidance.

Carajas, which is located in Brazil's Para state, is Vale's largest iron ore production complex. It produced 177.5 million metric tons last year, or more than half of the firm's total output of the steel ingredient in the period.

Vale said on Friday it expects iron ore production at Carajas to reach 200 million tons per year by 2030, with the boost coming from moves such as the expansion of its Serra Sul mine.

The miner also sees its copper production at Carajas growing by 32% to about 350,000 tons by 2030.

Vale will hold an event with Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva later on Friday to provide more details on the investment plan.

($1 = 5.7110 reais)

data.title: Udemy jumps on Q4 results beat
data.description:
** Shares of online learning platform firm Udemy rise ~31% to a nine-month high of $10.22

** On Thursday, company posted Q4 sales of $199.9 million, above analysts' average estimate of $194.7 million - data compiled by LSEG

** Posts Q4 adjusted profit of 10 cents/shr, also above the estimate of 7 cents/shr

** Forecasts 2025 revenue to be between $787 million and $803 million, compared with analysts' estimate of $798.7 million

** Expects Q1 revenue to be between $195 million and $199 million, compared with the estimate of $197.7 million

** Shares of Udemy fell ~41% in 2024

data.title: COMMENT-US recap:EUR/USD tops 1.05 as USD falls for fourth day
data.description:
The dollar index fell for a fourth day on Friday, sliding as tariff concerns eased while U.S growth worries emerged.

Treasury 10-year yields tumbled to 4.45%, reversing gains since the January payrolls a week ago, after a report that retail sales fell by most in nearly two years.

EUR/USD rose as high as 1.0514, fueled partly by optimism about Ukraine peace discussions. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said he was prepared to talk to President Vladimir Putin once Ukraine had agreed on a common plan with U.S. President Donald Trump and European leaders. Britain's foreign minister David Lammy said that he and U.S. Vice President JD Vance agreed Zelenskiy must be part of any peace talks.

Separately, the European Commission said it would react "firmly and immediately" against tariff increases.

USD/CHF fell below its 55-day moving average to a new year-to-date low of 0.8969.

Large 1.05 expiries and a nearby upper Bollinger slowed EUR/USD's ascent before reaching its year-to-date high at 1.0532. Options see upward momentum possibly fading with risk reversals still favoring euro puts.

Dollar weakness sent GBP/USD to a new year-to-date high of 1.2631, testing its nearby upper Bollinger. Momentum is currently working in the pound’s favor. A slew of U.K. economic reports will be eyed next week including employment and CPI. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has discussed visiting the United States on a call with Donald Trump.

USD/JPY was pressured by falling Treasury yields though downward momentum stalled before it reached the key 152 level where options are stationed. Volatilities and skews fell across tenors suggesting the pair, while remaining in a bearish trend, may not move far from its 200-DMA at 152.74. Resistance is seen at its 100-DMA at 153.35 and 21-DMA of 154.16.

Treasury yields were down 3 to 6 basis points. The 2s-10s curve was down less than 1 basis point at +22.0bp.

The S&P 500 rose 0.10% as yields eased.

WTI oil fell 0.70% on expectations that a Ukraine peace deal would unleash global supply.

Gold fell 1.56% in a round of profit-taking while copper was little changed.

Heading toward the close: EUR/USD +0.35%, USD/JPY -0.30%, GBP/USD +0.23%, AUD/USD +0.65%, =USD -0.36%, EUR/JPY +0.04%, GBP/JPY -0.06%, AUD/JPY +0.33%.

For more click on

data.title: Diamondback in talks to buy Double Eagle for more than $5 billion, source says
data.description:
Diamondback Energy is in talks to buy EnCap-backed Double Eagle, a big West Texas energy producer, for more than $5 billion, a person familiar with the matter said on Friday.

Last year, Reuters reported that Double Eagle was exploring a sale of its Permian Basin-based producer in a deal that could be worth more than $6.5 billion, including debt.

A deal could be announced in the coming days, if the talks don't fall apart, and it is also possible another suitor could emerge, the person said.

In September, Diamondback acquired Endeavor Energy Resources for $26 billion to create an oil and gas company valued at more than $50 billion.

Diamondback and Double Eagle did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

The Wall Street Journal reported on the talks first on Friday.

data.title: Why Dividend Stocks Should Be Your Valentine This Year — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Teresa Rivas

Roses are red; violets are blue. Headlines are crazy, but dividends stay true.

Income-hungry investors, after all, tend to crave stability. That resource might seem scarce in recent weeks, given the rapid-fire changes out of Washington and fears that the U.S. could cede its role as an artificial-intelligence leader. But research shows that the U.S. remains one of the best places for steady dividend payouts, and the dividends still serve as an excellent proxy for profits.

This Valentine's Day, it's hard to name a more iconic duo than U.S. companies' dividend increases and profit growth.

"Real world experience supports" the corporate finance theory that dividends are strong tells about management's optimism regarding the sustainability of earnings power, writes DataTrek Research Co-founder Nicholas Colas. There are no shortage of examples of big management shake-ups following dividend cuts, which are symptomatic of broader problems. By contrast, a steadily rising dividend is a sign that things are going well at a company.

"The same signaling mechanism applies to broad market indices," he writes. "Ideally, you want to see dividend payouts rise over time, just as would be the case with a healthy and growing individual company."

Of course, skeptics might be disheartened by the market's rocky relationship with dividend-paying stocks' lately, driven by higher interest rates, meaning the SPDR S&P Dividend exchange-traded fund has badly lagged behind the S&P 500 in the past year. (Why risk the volatility of a stock when bonds offer juicy yields?) Plus investors have interpreted Chinese AI firm DeepSeek's emergence as a threat to dividend-paying tech stocks.

But investors can still believe in the power of dividend stocks. After all, the fixed income market has its problems too — over the past 10 years, long-term Treasuries have posted a negative return for the first time in 90 years. There also are plenty of havens among domestic dividend payers from uncertain government policies like tariffs.

Most importantly, large U.S. public companies have delivered much stronger dividend growth than their non-U.S. peers over the past five years, which went hand-in-hand with better performance according to DataTrek's look at the performance of popular domestic and foreign ETFs. The range runs from a compound annual dividend growth rate of about 5% for the iShares Core S&P 500 ETF that tracks its namesake index to nearly 14% for the tech-heavy Invesco QQQ Trust. Their returns have outperformed overseas peers, too.

Still, it's a nuanced metric. Colas highlights that the equal-weighted S&P 500 and MSCI Japan have better dividend growth — nearly 7% and 9%, respectively — than the S&P 500's 5%, but worse five-year performance.

That's because investors still prize dividend growth from "growthier" companies over mature ones.

"The issue is one of sustainability. A tech stock with 5% dividend growth is more likely to outperform a consumer packaged goods company with 7% payout growth," Colas says.

Of course the equal-weight S&P 500 vastly diminishes the impact of Big Tech, many of which pay dividends. Japan is a unique case too: MSCI Japan is the only ETF whose five-year compound annual return trails its dividend growth over the same period. That shows how skeptical investors are of that nation's long-term growth, Colas notes.

Ultimately, higher dividend growth from the U.S. is another arrow in the quiver of the bull case for domestic stocks, Colas writes.

"We are long-term bullish on U.S. stocks over rest of world equities because their record of increasing hard cash dividend payments while still dominating global tech-enabled goods/services and growing earnings is second to none."

In fact, that's why Colas isn't concerned that some international ETFs — those tracking the MSCI Europe and emerging market indexes are outperforming the S&P 500 so far this year. He says that doesn't indicate any longer-term shift away from U.S. dominance.

"The American economy has been resilient and U.S. companies of all stripes (not just Tech) do a better job of generating strong equity market returns than their foreign counterparts," he concludes. "We remain positive on U.S. stocks."

That seems like good advice, even as the newsflow seems anything but steady-eddy. Love may be hard, but good dividends don't have to be.

Write to Teresa Rivas at teresa.rivas@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: TSMC considering running Intel's US factories after Trump team request, Bloomberg reports
data.description: Contract manufacturer TSMC is considering running chipmaker Intel's U.S. factories after requests from U.S. President Donald Trump's team, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

data.title: Economic Data Leave Equities Mixed Intraday
data.description:
US benchmark equity indexes were mixed intraday as markets parsed data showing a drop in retail sales and a rise in industrial production in January.

The Nasdaq Composite was up 0.3% at 20,000.3 after midday Friday, while the S&P 500 rose 0.1% to 6,119.8. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 0.2% to 44,622.5. Among sectors, financials and energy paced the gainers, while consumer staples saw the biggest drop.

In economic news, retail sales in the US fell more than market expectations last month amid declines in most categories, including the motor vehicle component, data from the Census Bureau showed.

"A resilient labor market, strong household balance sheets, and lower interest rates than a year ago mean we still expect consumer spending to rise at a solid pace in 2025," Oxford Economics said. "The proliferation of tariff threats takes off some of the shine, but are grounds for us to cut, not slash the forecast."

US industrial production increased more than projected in January amid gains in aerospace equipment and utilities, Federal Reserve data showed.

"Despite the monthly vagaries of the data, factory conditions are trending in the right direction," Oxford Economics said in a separate note.

The US two-year yield was down 5.8 basis points at 4.25% intraday, while the 10-year rate dropped 5.1 basis points to 4.47%.

In company news, Airbnb shares surged nearly 15%, the best performer on the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq. Late Thursday, the vacation rental company logged a bigger-than-projected fourth-quarter profit as strong demand propelled revenue above Wall Street's estimates.

Wynn Resorts was the second-top gainer on the S&P 500 intraday Friday, up 11%. The company late Thursday delivered a fourth-quarter beat.

Applied Materials shares dropped 6.8% intraday Friday, the steepest decline on the Nasdaq and among the worst on the S&P 500. Late Thursday, the semiconductor equipment manufacturer issued a downbeat fiscal second-quarter revenue outlook at the midpoint amid the impact of US restrictions on exports to China.

Magna International's US-listed shares decreased 4.4% after the Canadian auto parts supplier provided a full-year revenue outlook that fell short of the Street's expectations amid likely weakness in vehicle production.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil was down 0.7% at $70.75 a barrel intraday.

Gold fell 1.7% to $2,896.10 per troy ounce, while silver was little changed at $32.73 per ounce.

data.title: Henkel Product Winners: Product of the Year(TM) 2025
data.description:
Dial®, Persil®, and Schwarzkopf® brands win U.S.A Product of the Year™ awards

NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / February 14, 2025 / Product of the Year™ (POY) USA is the world's largest consumer-voted award for product innovation. The purpose of the award is to guide consumers to the best products in their market and reward manufacturers for quality and innovation. Each year, POY accepts entries for consumer products that demonstrate advancement within their industries. The POY USA seal is backed by a survey of 40,000 people by Kantar, a global leader in product innovation research.

Congratulations to the Dial®, Persil®, and Schwarzkopf® brands for winning 2025 Product of the Year USA Awards!

Dial® Healthy Hydration Body Wash - Winner in the Body Wash Category

The new Dial® Healthy Hydration Body Wash offers a unique combination of deep cleansing and effective hydration. This innovative formula leaves skin feeling clean and soft after just one shower. Healthy Hydration Body Wash contains ingredients such as Shea Butter Extract, Coconut Milk, and Hyaluronic Acid. With 50% more moisturizing ingredients (compared to Dial® Spring Water Body Wash), Dial® Healthy Hydration supports the brand's commitment to helping consumers feel confident and connected, all while "Dialing Up" their clean with every wash. Additionally, Dial® is proud to be Dermatologist Tested, PH Balanced, Paraben-Free and approved as cruelty free under the Leaping Bunny program. With a fresh lineup of launches for both men and women, Dial® brand is ready to resonate deeply with consumers and redefine the way they connect.

"It is an honor to have Dial®Healthy Hydration endorsed by Product of the Year as a 2025 winner for the Body Wash category," said Allison Feldman, Vice President of Marketing at Henkel. "At Dial®, we're committed to delivering products that not only give an undeniable clean but are also empowering. We learned from consumer insights that our audience is looking for body wash that provides both a thorough clean and effective hydration. We're very proud of this new Healthy Hydration range that will make high-quality personal care products accessible to everyone. We are excited to utilize the widely recognized and credible Product of the Year seal within upcoming marketing and sales efforts to guide shoppers towards a choice that 40,000 real consumers have found reliable and innovative."

Persil® Activewear Clean - Winner in the Laundry Category

Specifically designed for athletic wear while also working on all fabrics, Persil® Activewear Clean fights body

oil and sweat and eliminates odors, all while helping maintain fabric shape and stretch. The product is a first-of-its-kind variant for the brand, and was launched along with reimagined branding and additional variants in 2024.

"At Persil, we recognize that our consumers place high value in their clothes and, with the growth of athletic wear and synthetic clothing in recent years, we saw an opportunity to fill a gap in the laundry aisle and offer a solution for clothing that many people wear, day to day," said Matt Kutnick, Marketing Director of Persil. "Persil Activewear Clean helps maintain the delicate shape and stretch of performance wear while helping keep whites and colors vibrant so consumers can look and feel their best, wash after wash. For us, it's about providing a quality clean that also cares for the clothes our consumers love."

Schwarzkopf® Keratin Root Permanent Color and Keratin Root Temporary Color & Thickening Spray - Winner in the Beauty Category

The Schwarzkopf® Keratin Root Permanent Color is a permanent color treatment that refreshes the root of the hair by providing 100% gray coverage in just 10 minutes, leaving hair looking strong and healthy. The Schwarzkopf® Keratin Root Permanent Color kit contains the brand's exclusive bond enforcing system and a salon inspired 3-sided brush for precise application.

Schwarzkopf® Keratin Root Temporary Color & Thickening Spray with Keratin and Vitamin E is a temporary root color spray that instantly delivers up to 100% gray coverage and is scalp friendly. The formula is non-irritating and ammonia-free, and the non-sticky hold formula is delivered evenly and finely. This formulation helps ensure precise color application without mixing or mess that dries quickly and will wash out with your next shampoo.

"It is an honor to have Schwarzkopf® Keratin Root Permanent Color and Keratin Root Temporary Color & Thickening Spray endorsed by 2025 Product of the Year winners," said Christopher McClement, Senior Vice President, Marketing at Henkel Consumer Brands USA. "Evolution has been constant as the Schwarzkopf® brand has grown over the last century and our new range of Keratin Root products are a result of continuous research and development in an effort to elevate the hair care products and formulas we provide to our consumers. We are proud that this distinction - and the widely-recognized Product of The Year red seal used in connection with our products - will now be a symbol to consumers of Schwarzkopf Keratin Root's credible, reliable, and innovative hair color products, which they are sure to love."

View additional multimedia and more ESG storytelling from Henkel on 3blmedia.com.

Website: https://www.3blmedia.com/profiles/henkel

Email: info@3blmedia.com

data.title: Ben & Jerry's Accuses Parent Unilever of Banning Critical Trump Posts
data.description:
Unilever's Ben & Jerry's unit accused the parent company of blocking messages on social activism, banning posts critical of President Donald Trump.

In a Thursday letter to Judge Kevin Castel of the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York, Ben & Jerry's alleged Peter ter Kulve, Unilever's president of ice cream, unilaterally prohibited criticism of Trump until further review.

The executive said plans this year to restructure the ice cream business allegedly created a "new dynamic" in an "unprecedented time," according to the letter.

Unilever didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.
